logic_errors.description: Face render pass attempts to use bind group 4 but pipeline layout only defines groups 0-3. Additionally, group 3 is incorrectly assigned cameraBindGroup instead of polytopeBindGroup.
logic_errors.evidence:
Pipeline layout at lines 127-135 defines 4 bind groups [cameraBindGroupLayout, cameraBindGroupLayout, cameraBindGroupLayout, polytopeBindGroupLayout] but execute() calls:
  facePassEncoder.setBindGroup(0, this.cameraBindGroup)
  facePassEncoder.setBindGroup(1, this.cameraBindGroup) // placeholder
  facePassEncoder.setBindGroup(2, this.cameraBindGroup) // placeholder
  facePassEncoder.setBindGroup(3, this.cameraBindGroup) // WRONG - should be polytopeBindGroup per layout
  facePassEncoder.setBindGroup(4, this.polytopeBindGroup) // INVALID - group 4 doesn't exist

steps: 2. Call execute() with valid geometry data
3. WebGPU validation will throw error: 'Bind group index 4 exceeds pipeline layout bind group count 4'
reproduction.actual: WebGPU validation error - bind group index out of bounds

logic_errors.fix:
Change lines 630-634 to:
  facePassEncoder.setBindGroup(0, this.cameraBindGroup)
  facePassEncoder.setBindGroup(1, this.cameraBindGroup) // placeholder
  facePassEncoder.setBindGroup(2, this.cameraBindGroup) // placeholder
  facePassEncoder.setBindGroup(3, this.polytopeBindGroup) // polytope uniforms + basis

logic_errors.description: Edge render pass has identical bind group mismatch as face render pass - attempts to use bind group 4 but pipeline layout only defines groups 0-3.
logic_errors.evidence:
Same pattern as face pass:
  edgePassEncoder.setBindGroup(0, this.cameraBindGroup)
  edgePassEncoder.setBindGroup(1, this.cameraBindGroup) // placeholder
  edgePassEncoder.setBindGroup(2, this.cameraBindGroup) // placeholder
  edgePassEncoder.setBindGroup(3, this.cameraBindGroup) // WRONG
  edgePassEncoder.setBindGroup(4, this.polytopeBindGroup) // INVALID

steps: 1. Create a WebGPUPolytopeRenderer with edges: true
2. Call execute() with valid edge geometry
3. WebGPU validation throws same error for edge pass
reproduction.actual: WebGPU validation error - bind group index out of bounds

logic_errors.fix:
Change lines 668-672 to:
  edgePassEncoder.setBindGroup(0, this.cameraBindGroup)
  edgePassEncoder.setBindGroup(1, this.cameraBindGroup) // placeholder
  edgePassEncoder.setBindGroup(2, this.cameraBindGroup) // placeholder
  edgePassEncoder.setBindGroup(3, this.polytopeBindGroup) // polytope uniforms + basis

logic_errors.description: Uniform buffer is created with 128 bytes but data written is 160 bytes (Float32Array(40)). This will cause buffer overflow or WebGPU validation error.
logic_errors.evidence:
Line 396: this.uniformBuffer = this.createUniformBuffer(device, 128, 'screen-space-lensing-uniforms')
Line 659: const data = new Float32Array(40) // 160 bytes
Line 692: this.writeUniformBuffer(this.device, this.uniformBuffer, data)

Shader Uniforms struct requires:
- Scalars: 52 bytes (vec2f + 10 f32s)
- resolution + _pad: 16 bytes at offset 56
- inverseViewProjection: 64 bytes at offset 80 (16-byte aligned)
- cameraPosition + _pad2: 16 bytes at offset 144
Total: 160 bytes minimum

steps: 2. Call execute() which writes 160 bytes to 128-byte buffer
3. WebGPU will either throw validation error or corrupt adjacent memory
reproduction.actual: Buffer overflow error or undefined behavior

logic_errors.fix:
Change line 396 from:
  this.uniformBuffer = this.createUniformBuffer(device, 128, ...)
To:
  this.uniformBuffer = this.createUniformBuffer(device, 160, ...)

Alternatively, the comment at lines 394-395 already notes the correct size (160 bytes), so this appears to be a typo in the actual buffer creation.

verified_correct.aspect: Execution flow, pass ordering, command encoding, timestamp query handling, ping-pong buffer swapping
Resource cleanup in dispose() - properly destroys buffers and clears state
Bind group layout (5 entries) matches shader bindings, uniform buffer size (320 bytes) sufficient for 288-byte struct
Correct use of textureLoad for unfilterable-float depth texture (loadDepth function)
Bind group layout (4 entries) matches shader bindings, uniform buffer size (64 bytes) sufficient for 32-byte struct
Correct use of textureLoad for depth texture sampling in sampleBokeh function
Bind group layout (4 entries) matches shader bindings, uniform buffer size (256 bytes) sufficient for ~176-byte struct
Uniform buffer data layout correctly matches WGSL struct alignment requirements
Correct non-filtering sampler for unfilterable-float depth texture, proper uniform buffer layout
Correct unfilterable-float texture declaration, proper use of textureLoad for depth sampling
Bind group layout (5 entries) correctly separates filtering (color, normal) from unfilterable-float (depth)
Complex uniform buffer (352 bytes) correctly accounts for 4 matrices plus all jet parameters with proper alignment
Bind group layout (5 entries) correctly handles depth as unfilterable-float, proper shell glow implementation

semantic_issues.description: NormalPass declares sampler as 'filtering' but depth texture is unfilterable-float. While the code correctly uses textureLoad instead of textureSample, the sampler binding is never actually used. This is wasteful but not a bug.
semantic_issues.actually_does: Sampler is created but never used - all depth access uses textureLoad

checked_patterns: textureLoad used for unfilterable-float textures (depth buffers)
textureSample used only with filtering samplers on filterable textures
Uniform struct alignment follows WGSL rules (mat4x4f at 16-byte boundaries)
No dynamic indexing of uniform arrays that would require storage buffers
Proper use of @group and @binding decorators matching TypeScript bind group layouts
wgsl_shader_verification.notes: All WGSL shaders use modern WebGPU patterns correctly. Depth textures are properly handled as unfilterable-float with textureLoad() instead of textureSample().

summary.overall_assessment: The WebGPU port has 3 critical bugs that will cause runtime failures: (1) WebGPUPolytopeRenderer uses bind group index 4 but pipeline only defines 0-3, (2) same renderer assigns wrong bind group to index 3, (3) ScreenSpaceLensingPass has buffer size mismatch (128 vs 160 bytes). All other passes appear correctly implemented with proper WGSL alignment, texture sampling patterns, and resource management.
